# Questions in Livestream 154

Live Stream #154: A Brief Question of Logic

Time links open the YouTube video at the relevant timestamp.

| Time | Question | Short answer / answer direction |
|---:|---|---|
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=655" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">10:55</a> | Is there anything biblical archaeology is especially unlikely ever to discover? | He says the ark of the covenant is the clearest example and argues it was likely destroyed rather than waiting to be rediscovered. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=767" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">12:47</a> | Did ancient kings ever exile brothers instead of killing them as succession threats? | He says Egypt usually redirected brothers into administration or the military, unlike harsher Mesopotamian patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=1079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">17:59</a> | What kind of cross was Jesus crucified on? | He rules out a simple stake or X-shape and says it was probably between a Latin cross and a tau form. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=1277" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">21:17</a> | What criticisms do you have of postmodernism, especially social constructs and skeptical subjectivity? | He says postmodernity has some useful insights, but he rejects Marxist social-construct claims and radical skeptical subjectivity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=1544" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">25:44</a> | What are your thoughts on epistemic particularism? | He contrasts it with epistemic methodism and says particularism works well for innate or moral knowledge that does not first require formal justification. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=1769" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">29:29</a> | How can people know what is true if they cannot reach 100 percent certainty? | He argues people work with probability and confidence rather than absolute certainty, with faith bridging the remaining uncertainty. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=2078" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">34:38</a> | What are his thoughts on Heaven's Gate? | He treats it as a UFO-centered suicide cult and briefly sketches its origins and later survival. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=2286" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:06</a> | Does he have a black belt, and does he have any stories about it? | He says the answer is complicated, confirms there are stories, and declines to share them. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=2333" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">38:53</a> | What is love? | He says in Christian terms love is more a chosen act of doing good than merely a feeling. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=2702" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">45:02</a> | Which cults does he find most interesting? | He says the stranger groups are often most interesting to study and singles out vampire cults as especially fascinating. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=3331" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">55:31</a> | What books build strong arguments against major world religions? | He recommends first learning each religion from the inside through solid introductory and specific studies, then building arguments from that understanding. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=3603" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:00:03</a> | What is the meaning of Ephesians 3:8-10? | He says the hidden mystery of God's gospel is now unveiled universally to Jew and Gentile through the church. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=4215" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:10:15</a> | Where can someone find scholarly articles and keep up with excavations in Egypt and the Levant? | He says the latest work is in academic journals, especially relevant society journals, though they are expensive and sometimes mirrored later on Academia.edu. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=4480" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:14:40</a> | Was Paul's apostleship only like the seventy in Luke 10, and how can Paul's canonicity be defended? | He says Luke 10 names "others," not apostles, and points to apostolic recognition such as 2 Peter's treatment of Paul's writings. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=5292" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:28:12</a> | How were ancient hours affected by seasonal daylight, and why was lamb's blood used for Passover? | He says daylight and nighttime were each divided into twelve variable-length hours, and lamb blood was used because lambs were widely accessible. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=5574" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:32:54</a> | Which pharaohs did Moses live through, and does the Bible imply only two pharaohs in Moses' time? | He says the text does not require only two; it uses literary amalgamation for oppression pharaohs and one exodus pharaoh. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=6798" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:53:18</a> | Is there a logical problem with deconstruction? | He says deconstruction is not really a logical system so much as a broader way of approaching life and meaning. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=6895" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:54:55</a> | How do we know Job is Iron Age literature? | He says Job's references to Arameans fit the period after Mitanni's collapse and before Aram fully reformed. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=7029" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">1:57:09</a> | What are his thoughts on The Action Bible? | He dislikes reducing the Bible to entertainment value, even though he is not against comic-book formats as such. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=7385" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:03:05</a> | What is a discernment ministry? | He says it is a ministry focused on distinguishing truth from error, but warns that such ministries can fall into caricature. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=8648" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:08</a> | How often did kings kill their brothers? | He says it depended on the culture, with Assyrian, Babylonian, and Ottoman examples much harsher than Egyptian patterns. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=8688" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:24:48</a> | What does he mean by saying eunuchs were crushed rather than castrated? | He says some ancient Near Eastern eunuchs were made by a crushing procedure because full castration carried a high infection risk. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=9426" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">2:37:06</a> | What is a consecutive vav, and how does Stephen rely on it in Acts? | He says a consecutive vav marks temporal progression, and Stephen reads Genesis 11-12 as Abraham leaving after Terah's death. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=11137" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:05:37</a> | Who is Agur son of Jakeh in Proverbs 30? | He says the text gives too little information; Agur may be a collector or compiler of proverbs, but his identity is unknown. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=11206" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:06:46</a> | How would he respond to someone saying Jesus went to hell to get the keys from Satan? | He rejects the claim, distinguishing Hades from hell and saying Satan had no keys or rightful title to humanity. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=11781" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:16:21</a> | Under historic premillennialism, is there a necessary distinction between the church and Israel? | He says the distinction has often appeared historically, but he does not see it as necessary to the view. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=12043" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:20:43</a> | How should someone seeking assurance respond to thoughts of ending life to see whether Christ is real? | He says ending one's life is never the answer and points instead to living by faith and serving Christ. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=12504" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:28:24</a> | Does self-titling as a prophet or apostle disqualify someone as a source of doctrine? | He says not automatically, but it should raise concern; doctrine and context still have to be judged. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=13133" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:38:53</a> | What is the second most reasonable explanation for Israelite origins if the exodus is rejected? | He says a later Amorite infiltration may be the next option, but only after ignoring a great deal of Egyptian-pattern evidence. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=14338" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">3:58:58</a> | How much of the Bible is against gnosticism? | He says relatively little directly addresses gnosticism; the New Testament touches it only tangentially, while the church fathers engage it more. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=14566" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:02:46</a> | Would people from different cultures report different things from the same ghost sighting? | He says yes, cultural context can shape details even when people report the same event. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=14920" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:08:40</a> | Did kings also kill their sisters? | He says generally no, because sisters were less direct succession threats and could serve dynastic marriage purposes. |
| <a href="https://youtu.be/kH3qHY_b3BU?t=15079" target="_blank" rel="noopener noreferrer">4:11:19</a> | Did women also get beatings in Egypt? | He says yes; Egyptian authorities could beat women as well as men, including high-status women in legal contexts. |
